    97→
    98→## Architecture Patterns
    99→
   100→### Recommended File Layout
   101→
   102→```
   103→libs/model-training/src/model_training/
   104→├── sakana_d2l.py        # ADD: transfer_aggregator_weights(), get_aggregator_config(),
   105→│                        #      _assert_transfer_integrity()
   106→├── d2l_config.py        # UPDATE: build_qwen3_hypernet_config() accepts aggregator_config
   107→│                        #         OR document that caller must set it after calling
   108→│                        #         get_aggregator_config()
   109→└── __init__.py          # (no change needed — GPU deps not exported at top level)
   110→
   111→libs/model-training/tests/
   112→└── test_d2l_weight_transfer.py   # NEW: tests for transfer, freezing, assertions
   113→```
   114→
   115→Adding to `sakana_d2l.py` is preferred over a new module because:
   116→- All HyperLoRA checkpoint logic already lives there
   117→- `download_checkpoint()` and `load_sakana_checkpoint()` are the natural callees
   118→- No new import surface to maintain
   119→
   120→### Pattern 1: Partial State Dict Transfer (strict=False)
   121→
   122→**What:** Build a filtered state dict containing only `aggregator.*` keys, then call `load_state_dict(strict=False)`. Inspect the returned `_IncompatibleKeys` namedtuple.
   123→
   124→**When to use:** Any time you want to transfer a subset of a checkpoint into a larger or differently-sized model.
   125→
   126→**Implementation:**
   127→```python
   128→# Source: extends existing sakana_d2l.py lines 211-229 pattern
   129→import torch  # noqa: PLC0415
   130→
   131→def transfer_aggregator_weights(
   132→    hypernet: Any,           # HyperLoRA instance built for Qwen3-Coder-Next
   133→    checkpoint_path: str | Path,
   134→) -> Any:                    # returns the same hypernet (mutated in-place)
   135→    sd = torch.load(str(checkpoint_path), map_location="cpu", weights_only=False)
   136→
   137→    # Filter: aggregator keys that also exist in the target model
   138→    aggregator_sd = {
   139→        k: v for k, v in sd.items()
   140→        if k.startswith("aggregator.") and k in hypernet.state_dict()
   141→    }
   142→
   143→    loaded = hypernet.load_state_dict(aggregator_sd, strict=False)
   144→
   145→    # Assertion: aggregator must be fully loaded
   146→    _assert_transfer_integrity(hypernet, loaded)
   147→
   148→    # Freeze aggregator parameters
   149→    for name, param in hypernet.named_parameters():
   150→        if name.startswith("aggregator."):
   151→            param.requires_grad_(False)
   152→
   153→    return hypernet
   154→```
   155→
   156→**Key insight:** `strict=False` returns `_IncompatibleKeys(missing_keys=[...], unexpected_keys=[...])`. `missing_keys` = keys in the model not present in the supplied state dict (i.e., `head.*` — expected). `unexpected_keys` = keys in the state dict not present in the model (should be empty for aggregator-only transfer).
   157→
   158→### Pattern 2: Post-Load Integrity Assertion
   159→
   160→**What:** After `strict=False` loading, assert that: (1) no aggregator key is missing, (2) all missing keys are `head.*`, (3) no unexpected keys exist.
   161→
   162→**When to use:** Always after partial weight transfer — fail hard rather than silently using randomly-initialized aggregator weights.
   163→
   164→```python
   165→# Source: derived from CONTEXT.md assertion strategy
   166→def _assert_transfer_integrity(
   167→    hypernet: Any,
   168→    loaded: Any,   # _IncompatibleKeys namedtuple from load_state_dict()
   169→) -> None:
   170→    # 1. All head.* parameters must be in missing_keys (not loaded from checkpoint)
   171→    for key in loaded.missing_keys:
   172→        if not key.startswith("head."):
   173→            msg = (
   174→                f"Unexpected missing key after aggregator transfer: '{key}'. "
   175→                "Expected only head.* keys to be missing."
   176→            )
   177→            raise AssertionError(msg)
   178→
   179→    # 2. No unexpected keys (aggregator keys not recognized by model)
   180→    if loaded.unexpected_keys:
   181→        msg = (
   182→            f"Unexpected keys after aggregator transfer: {loaded.unexpected_keys}. "
   183→            "These keys were in the checkpoint but not in the model."
   184→        )
   185→        raise AssertionError(msg)
   186→
   187→    # 3. Every aggregator.* key in the model must have been loaded
   188→    model_aggregator_keys = {
   189→        k for k in hypernet.state_dict() if k.startswith("aggregator.")
   190→    }
   191→    missing_aggregator = {k for k in loaded.missing_keys if k.startswith("aggregator.")}
   192→    if missing_aggregator:
   193→        msg = (
   194→            f"Aggregator keys missing after transfer: {missing_aggregator}. "
   195→            "The checkpoint may use different key prefixes — print checkpoint.keys() to verify."
   196→        )
   197→        raise AssertionError(msg)
   198→
   199→    logger.info(
   200→        "Transfer integrity OK: %d aggregator keys loaded, %d head keys re-initialized",
   201→        len(model_aggregator_keys),
   202→        len(loaded.missing_keys),
   203→    )
   204→```
   205→
   206→### Pattern 3: requires_grad Freezing and Verification
   207→
   208→**What:** After loading, iterate `named_parameters()` and call `requires_grad_(False)` on every `aggregator.*` param. Verify afterward.
   209→
   210→```python
   211→# Source: standard PyTorch pattern — verified via existing project code
   212→# Freeze
   213→for name, param in hypernet.named_parameters():
   214→    if name.startswith("aggregator."):
   215→        param.requires_grad_(False)
   216→
   217→# Verify (in test or assertion)
   218→for name, param in hypernet.named_parameters():
   219→    if name.startswith("aggregator."):
   220→        assert not param.requires_grad, f"Expected frozen: {name}"
   221→    elif name.startswith("head."):
   222→        assert param.requires_grad, f"Expected trainable: {name}"
   223→```
   224→
   225→**Critical:** Call `param.requires_grad_(False)` NOT `param.requires_grad = False`. The `_()` in-place method is the PyTorch-idiomatic form and correctly propagates through the optimizer.
   226→
   227→### Pattern 4: get_aggregator_config()
   228→
   229→**What:** Inspect the loaded checkpoint's `hypernet_config` (a `HypernetConfig` stored in the `.bin` file under key `"hypernet_config"`) to extract the aggregator sub-config for use in `build_qwen3_hypernet_config()`.
   230→
   231→```python
   232→# Source: sakana_d2l.py line 199 — checkpoint stores sd["hypernet_config"]
   233→def get_aggregator_config(checkpoint_path: str | Path) -> Any:
   234→    import torch  # noqa: PLC0415
   235→    sd = torch.load(str(checkpoint_path), map_location="cpu", weights_only=False)
   236→    hc = sd["hypernet_config"]
   237→    # HypernetConfig has aggregator_config field from the source checkpoint
   238→    return hc.aggregator_config
   239→```
   240→
   241→**Why:** The `qwen_4b_d2l` checkpoint's `HypernetConfig` carries the Perceiver's structural config in `aggregator_config`. By reading it from the source checkpoint, Phase 27 avoids hardcoding Perceiver hyperparameters and ensures the target `HypernetConfig` is built with matching aggregator structure.
   242→
   243→### Pattern 5: Test Mock Strategy for Checkpoint Loading
   244→
   245→**What:** The `torch.load()` call inside `transfer_aggregator_weights()` cannot run in CI without a real `.bin` file. Use `monkeypatch` (pytest) to replace `torch.load` with a function returning a fake checkpoint dict.
   246→
   247→```python
   248→# Source: project test pattern (sys.modules injection + monkeypatch)
   249→import torch
   250→import torch.nn as nn
   251→
   252→class _FakeAggregatorModel(nn.Module):
   253→    """Minimal HyperLoRA mock with aggregator.* and head.* parameters."""
   254→    def __init__(self) -> None:
   255→        super().__init__()
   256→        self.aggregator = nn.Linear(4, 4)   # aggregator.weight, aggregator.bias
   257→        self.head = nn.Linear(4, 12)         # head.weight, head.bias
   258→
   259→def _make_fake_checkpoint(model: nn.Module) -> dict:
   260→    """Build a fake checkpoint dict matching real structure."""
   261→    sd = model.state_dict()
   262→    # Only include aggregator keys in the checkpoint
   263→    agg_sd = {k: v for k, v in sd.items() if k.startswith("aggregator.")}
   264→    return {
   265→        "hypernet_config": None,  # or a MagicMock
   266→        **agg_sd,
   267→    }
   268→```
   269→
   270→The monkeypatch replaces `torch.load` at the call site, or the test calls `transfer_aggregator_weights()` directly with a pre-built state dict via a helper that exposes the inner logic.
   271→
   272→**Alternative (preferred):** Extract the state dict building into a helper that accepts either a path OR a pre-built dict, making it testable without filesystem/GPU.
   273→
   274→### Anti-Patterns to Avoid
   275→
   276→- **`strict=True` for partial transfer:** Will raise `RuntimeError` because `head.*` keys exist in the model but not the checkpoint. Always use `strict=False` for partial transfer.
   277→- **Iterating `model.parameters()` to freeze (not `named_parameters()`):** Cannot filter by name. Use `named_parameters()`.
   278→- **Setting `param.requires_grad = False` as attribute assignment:** Works in PyTorch but `requires_grad_(False)` is idiomatic and avoids leaf tensor confusion.
   279→- **Not asserting on `missing_keys`:** If aggregator keys are renamed between checkpoint versions, the transfer silently uses random init for all weights. The assertion catches this.
   280→- **Loading `head.*` from the old checkpoint:** The old head was sized for Qwen-4B's layers. Loading it would give wrong output dimensions for Qwen3-Coder-Next's 12 layers. The key filtering (`startswith("aggregator.")`) prevents this.
   281→- **Calling `hypernet.eval()` before freezing:** `.eval()` does not freeze parameters — it only affects BatchNorm/Dropout behavior. Freezing requires `requires_grad_(False)`.
   282→
   283→---
   284→
   285→## Don't Hand-Roll
   286→
   287→| Problem | Don't Build | Use Instead | Why |
   288→|---------|-------------|-------------|-----|
   289→| Partial state dict loading | Custom weight-copying loop | `load_state_dict(strict=False)` | Returns `missing_keys`/`unexpected_keys` for free; handles tensor shape mismatch with clear error |
   290→| Parameter freezing | Custom optimizer param filter | `param.requires_grad_(False)` | PyTorch native; optimizer automatically skips requires_grad=False params |
   291→| Key namespace discovery | Guessing from docs | `torch.load()` + print checkpoint keys | CONTEXT.md explicitly flags this as a blocker — do it in Wave 0 |
   292→| Checkpoint download | Custom HF download | `download_checkpoint(variant="qwen_4b_d2l")` already in sakana_d2l.py | Already implemented and cached |
   293→
   294→**Key insight:** `load_state_dict(strict=False)` is the entire mechanism. The phase is really about wrapping it with the right assertions and freeze loop — not building novel loading logic.
   295→
   296→---
   297→
   298→## Common Pitfalls
   299→
   300→### Pitfall 1: Unknown Checkpoint Key Namespaces
   301→
   302→**What goes wrong:** The code in `sakana_d2l.py` lines 212-215 filters for `aggregator.`, `head.`, and `extra_` prefixes, but this was inferred from the code, not verified against the actual `qwen_4b_d2l` checkpoint. If the real checkpoint uses different prefixes (e.g., `perceiver.` instead of `aggregator.`), the transfer loads zero weights and the assertion fails.
   303→
   304→**Why it happens:** `qwen_4b_d2l` and `gemma_demo` may use different key conventions. The existing code paths primarily exercised `gemma_demo`.
   305→
   306→**How to avoid:** Wave 0 task: load the checkpoint with `torch.load()` and print/log the key prefixes. Implement the transfer only after confirming the actual prefix. The `_assert_transfer_integrity()` function will catch any remaining mismatch.
   307→
   308→**Warning signs:** `_assert_transfer_integrity` raises `AssertionError` about missing aggregator keys. The transfer loaded 0 weight tensors.
   309→
   310→### Pitfall 2: Head Shape Mismatch Between Source and Target
   311→
   312→**What goes wrong:** If any `head.*` key from the old checkpoint accidentally gets included in the partial state dict (e.g., a prefix filter bug), `load_state_dict` will raise a shape mismatch error because old head was sized for Qwen-4B's layers, not Qwen3-Coder-Next's 12 layers.
   313→
   314→**Why it happens:** The checkpoint filter must use `startswith("aggregator.")` only, not a broader prefix.
   315→
   316→**How to avoid:** The filter `k.startswith("aggregator.")` is precise. Verify in `_assert_transfer_integrity` that no `head.*` keys appear in `unexpected_keys` (they should be absent from the supplied state dict entirely, not rejected as unexpected).
   317→
   318→**Warning signs:** `load_state_dict` raises `RuntimeError: size mismatch for head.*` — this means `head.*` keys were accidentally included in the partial state dict.
   319→
   320→### Pitfall 3: requires_grad_(False) Not Applied to All Aggregator Parameters
   321→
   322→**What goes wrong:** If some aggregator parameters are in submodules of `hypernet.aggregator`, they appear in `named_parameters()` as `aggregator.resampler.layers.0.weight`, etc. A check like `if name == "aggregator"` misses these. The `startswith("aggregator.")` check is required.
   323→
   324→**Why it happens:** Deep module hierarchies — `aggregator` is itself a multi-layer Perceiver with nested parameters.
   325→
   326→**How to avoid:** Use `name.startswith("aggregator.")` in the freeze loop. After freezing, verify with a second iteration asserting `not param.requires_grad` for all aggregator params.
   327→
   328→**Warning signs:** `optimizer.param_groups` still includes aggregator params after freezing. Phase 29 training updates aggregator weights (should be frozen).
   329→
   330→### Pitfall 4: get_aggregator_config Returns None
   331→
   332→**What goes wrong:** If the `qwen_4b_d2l` checkpoint's `HypernetConfig` was built without an explicit `aggregator_config` (set to `None`), `get_aggregator_config()` returns `None`. The `d2l_config.py` `aggregator_config=None` placeholder would remain, causing `HyperLoRA` construction to fail in Phase 28/29.
   333→
   334→**Why it happens:** Older Sakana checkpoints may predatethe `aggregator_config` field being added to `HypernetConfig`.
   335→
   336→**How to avoid:** In the Wave 0 verification step, print `sd["hypernet_config"]` to confirm `aggregator_config` is populated. If it's `None`, raise an explicit error in `get_aggregator_config()` rather than returning `None` silently. Phase 28 depends on this being non-None.
   337→
   338→**Warning signs:** `get_aggregator_config()` returns `None`. `build_qwen3_hypernet_config()` called with `aggregator_config=None` hits the existing placeholder path.
   339→
   340→### Pitfall 5: torch.load weights_only=False Required
   341→
   342→**What goes wrong:** Using `torch.load(..., weights_only=True)` raises an error because the checkpoint contains `HypernetConfig` (a Python dataclass / object), not just tensors. PyTorch's `weights_only=True` mode restricts loading to tensor-safe objects only.
   343→
   344→**Why it happens:** The checkpoint stores arbitrary Python objects (`hypernet_config`, `base_model_name_or_path`) alongside tensors.
   345→
   346→**How to avoid:** Use `weights_only=False` as in the existing `load_sakana_checkpoint()` (line 197). This is a security trade-off (trusted checkpoint from HuggingFace). Document the reason with a comment.
   347→
   348→**Warning signs:** `_pickle.UnpicklingError` or `WeightsUnpickler` error on `torch.load`.
   349→
   350→---
   351→
   352→## Code Examples
   353→
   354→### Verified: Existing Checkpoint Loading Pattern (sakana_d2l.py lines 211-229)
   355→
   356→```python
   357→# Source: sakana_d2l.py lines 211-229 (existing — read 2026-03-13)
   358→hypernet_keys = [
   359→    k for k in sd.keys() if k.startswith(("aggregator.", "head.", "extra_"))
   360→]
   361→hypernet_sd = {k: sd[k] for k in hypernet_keys if k in hypernet.state_dict()}
   362→
   363→if hypernet_sd:
   364→    loaded = hypernet.load_state_dict(hypernet_sd, strict=False)
   365→    logger.info("Loaded %d perceiver weight tensors", len(hypernet_sd))
   366→    if loaded.missing_keys:
   367→        logger.info("Missing keys: %d", len(loaded.missing_keys))
   368→```
   369→
   370→Phase 27 narrows the filter to `aggregator.` only (not `head.` or `extra_`) and adds assertions.
   371→
   372→### Verified: requires_grad_(False) Pattern
   373→
   374→```python
   375→# Source: standard PyTorch API — confirmed in pytorch docs (no version change)
   376→# Freeze all aggregator parameters
   377→for name, param in hypernet.named_parameters():
   378→    if name.startswith("aggregator."):
   379→        param.requires_grad_(False)
   380→
   381→# Verification loop (use in test or assertion)
   382→frozen_count = 0
   383→trainable_count = 0
   384→for name, param in hypernet.named_parameters():
   385→    if name.startswith("aggregator."):
   386→        assert not param.requires_grad, f"Should be frozen: {name}"
   387→        frozen_count += 1
   388→    elif name.startswith("head."):
   389→        assert param.requires_grad, f"Should be trainable: {name}"
   390→        trainable_count += 1
   391→logger.info("Frozen: %d, trainable: %d", frozen_count, trainable_count)
   392→```
   393→
   394→### Verified: _IncompatibleKeys Structure
   395→
   396→```python
   397→# Source: PyTorch load_state_dict documentation / source
   398→# load_state_dict(strict=False) returns _IncompatibleKeys namedtuple:
   399→#   .missing_keys   — keys in model state_dict but NOT in supplied dict
   400→#   .unexpected_keys — keys in supplied dict but NOT in model state_dict
   401→loaded = hypernet.load_state_dict(aggregator_sd, strict=False)
   402→print(loaded.missing_keys)    # ["head.weight", "head.bias", ...]
   403→print(loaded.unexpected_keys) # [] (should be empty for correct transfer)
   404→```
   405→
   406→### Wave 0 Verification Script (run before implementing)
   407→
   408→```python
   409→# uv run python -c "..."  — verify checkpoint key namespaces
   410→import torch
   411→from pathlib import Path
   412→
   413→ckpt = Path.home() / ".cache/rune/sakana_d2l/qwen_4b_d2l/pytorch_model.bin"
   414→sd = torch.load(str(ckpt), map_location="cpu", weights_only=False)
   415→prefixes = sorted({k.split(".")[0] for k in sd if isinstance(sd[k], torch.Tensor)})
   416→print("Top-level tensor key prefixes:", prefixes)
   417→print("hypernet_config:", sd.get("hypernet_config"))
   418→```
   419→
   420→Expected output: `['aggregator', 'head']` (possibly `'extra'`). Any deviation from this changes the filter logic in `transfer_aggregator_weights()`.
   421→
   422→---
   423→
   424→## State of the Art
   425→
   426→| Old Approach | Current Approach | When Changed | Impact |
   427→|--------------|------------------|--------------|--------|
   428→| `load_sakana_checkpoint()` loads aggregator + head from matching checkpoint | `transfer_aggregator_weights()` loads aggregator only, re-initializes head for new target | Phase 27 | Enables reuse of Perceiver across different target model architectures |
   429→| `aggregator_config=None` placeholder in `build_qwen3_hypernet_config()` | `get_aggregator_config()` populates from loaded checkpoint | Phase 27 | HypernetConfig fully specified; Phase 28/29 can build and train |
   430→| `load_state_dict` with no post-load verification | `_assert_transfer_integrity()` asserts key completeness | Phase 27 | Fails hard on checkpoint/model mismatches rather than silently using random init |
   431→
   432→---
   433→
   434→## Open Questions
   435→
   436→1. **Exact checkpoint key prefixes for `qwen_4b_d2l`**
   437→   - What we know: Existing `sakana_d2l.py` code filters for `aggregator.`, `head.`, `extra_` prefixes. This was inferred, not verified.
   438→   - What's unclear: Whether `qwen_4b_d2l` uses exactly these prefixes or variations (e.g., `perceiver.`, `aggregator_model.`).
   439→   - Recommendation: First task in Wave 0 is to run the verification script above. Do not implement the transfer function until the actual prefixes are confirmed.
   440→
   441→2. **Whether `hypernet_config.aggregator_config` is non-None in `qwen_4b_d2l`**
   442→   - What we know: `HypernetConfig` has an `aggregator_config` field; `build_qwen3_hypernet_config()` currently sets it to `None`.
   443→   - What's unclear: Whether the saved `qwen_4b_d2l` checkpoint's `HypernetConfig` has `aggregator_config` populated.
   444→   - Recommendation: Print `sd["hypernet_config"]` in Wave 0 verification. If `None`, the `get_aggregator_config()` function must either extract the config by inspecting the aggregator module structure (derive from loaded weights) or document that it returns `None` and Phase 28 must handle that case.
   445→
   446→3. **Whether `HyperLoRA` built with `build_qwen3_hypernet_config()` has `aggregator.*` keys at all**
   447→   - What we know: `build_qwen3_hypernet_config()` constructs a `HypernetConfig` with `aggregator_config=None`. `HyperLoRA(hc)` instantiation may behave differently than `HyperLoRA` from the source checkpoint.
   448→   - What's unclear: Whether the Qwen3-targeted `HyperLoRA` has the same `aggregator.*` module structure as the `qwen_4b_d2l` one, or if `aggregator_config=None` causes the aggregator to be absent entirely.
   449→   - Recommendation: Wave 0 task includes printing `list(HyperLoRA(build_qwen3_hypernet_config()).state_dict().keys())` to confirm aggregator modules are present before attempting transfer.
   450→
   451→---
   452→
   453→## Sources
   454→
   455→### Primary (HIGH confidence)
   456→- `/Users/noahdolevelixir/Code/rune/libs/model-training/src/model_training/sakana_d2l.py` (read 2026-03-13) — existing `load_sakana_checkpoint()` implementation with `strict=False` pattern and `aggregator.`/`head.`/`extra_` filtering at lines 211-229
   457→- `/Users/noahdolevelixir/Code/rune/libs/model-training/src/model_training/d2l_config.py` (read 2026-03-13) — `build_qwen3_hypernet_config()` `aggregator_config=None` placeholder at line 142-143
   458→- `.planning/phases/27-partial-weight-transfer/27-CONTEXT.md` (read 2026-03-13) — all locked decisions
   459→- `.planning/STATE.md` (read 2026-03-13) — "qwen_4b_d2l checkpoint key namespaces inferred from code" blocker
   460→- PyTorch `load_state_dict(strict=False)` API — `_IncompatibleKeys(missing_keys, unexpected_keys)` return type is stable and documented
   461→
   462→### Secondary (MEDIUM confidence)
   463→- `/Users/noahdolevelixir/Code/rune/libs/model-training/tests/test_d2l_probe.py` (read 2026-03-13) — established `sys.modules` injection + tiny `nn.Module` mock pattern for CI tests without GPU deps
   464→- `/Users/noahdolevelixir/Code/rune/libs/model-training/tests/test_hypernetwork.py` (read 2026-03-13) — `_inject_fake_hn_modules()` pattern for `torch`/`safetensors` mocking
   465→
   466→### Tertiary (LOW confidence)
   467→- `qwen_4b_d2l` checkpoint key prefixes — INFERRED from existing code; not verified against actual `.bin` file. Confirmed as a STATE.md blocker requiring Wave 0 verification.
   468→- `hypernet_config.aggregator_config` population in `qwen_4b_d2l` checkpoint — not verified; depends on Sakana checkpoint version.

    55→- KL-divergence context distillation (not MSE on weights) — weight spaces have permutation symmetry; MSE is mathematically unsound

    58→- Task-ID-level train/test split before augmentation — prevents task-family leakage from augmented variants

1→"""SakanaAI Doc-to-LoRA integration.
     2→
     3→Wraps Sakana's pretrained HyperLoRA perceiver so it can be used through
     4→our hypernetwork interface (load_pretrained → generate_adapter).
     5→
     6→The Sakana hypernetwork takes per-layer activations from a base model as
     7→input and produces LoRA adapter weights.  This module handles:
     8→  - Downloading the checkpoint from HuggingFace
     9→  - Patching flash-attention assertions for CPU/MPS/non-flash environments
    10→  - Extracting per-layer activations from the base model
    11→  - Saving the generated LoRA weights in PEFT format
    12→
    13→GPU imports are deferred inside function bodies per INFRA-05 pattern.
    14→"""
    15→
    16→from __future__ import annotations
    17→
    18→import json
    19→import logging
    20→from pathlib import Path
    21→from typing import Any
    22→
    23→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    24→
    25→# HuggingFace repo for Sakana's pretrained checkpoints
    26→HF_REPO_ID = "SakanaAI/doc-to-lora"
    27→# Available checkpoints: gemma_2b_d2l, gemma_demo, mistral_7b_d2l, qwen_4b_d2l
    28→DEFAULT_VARIANT = "gemma_demo"
    29→DEFAULT_HF_FILENAME = f"{DEFAULT_VARIANT}/checkpoint-80000/pytorch_model.bin"
    30→LOCAL_CACHE_DIR = Path.home() / ".cache" / "rune" / "sakana_d2l"
    31→
    32→
    33→def _patch_flash_attention() -> None:
    34→    """Patch Sakana's idefics2 module to work without flash_attn.
    35→
    36→    Replaces flash attention classes and assertions with eager equivalents
    37→    so the perceiver can run on CPU/MPS/CUDA without flash_attn installed.
    38→    """
    39→    import ctx_to_lora.modeling.idefics2 as idefics2_mod  # noqa: PLC0415
    40→    import torch  # noqa: PLC0415
    41→    from ctx_to_lora.modeling.idefics2 import (  # noqa: PLC0415
    42→        Idefics2Perceiver,
    43→        Idefics2PerceiverAttention,
    44→        Idefics2PerceiverConfig,
    45→        Idefics2PerceiverResampler,
    46→    )
    47→
    48→    # Map both eager and flash_attention_2 to the eager attention class
    49→    idefics2_mod.IDEFICS2_PERCEIVER_ATTENTION_CLASSES["eager"] = (
    50→        Idefics2PerceiverAttention
    51→    )
    52→    idefics2_mod.IDEFICS2_PERCEIVER_ATTENTION_CLASSES["flash_attention_2"] = (
    53→        Idefics2PerceiverAttention
    54→    )
    55→
    56→    # Patch eager attention forward to accept flash-only kwargs
    57→    _orig_attn_fwd = Idefics2PerceiverAttention.forward
    58→
    59→    def _patched_attn_fwd(
    60→        self: Any, *args: Any, is_cross_attn: Any = None, **kwargs: Any
    61→    ) -> Any:
    62→        kwargs.pop("cu_seq_lens_q", None)
    63→        kwargs.pop("cu_seq_lens_k", None)
    64→        kwargs.pop("max_length_q", None)
    65→        kwargs.pop("max_length_k", None)
    66→        return _orig_attn_fwd(self, *args, **kwargs)
    67→
    68→    Idefics2PerceiverAttention.forward = _patched_attn_fwd
    69→
    70→    # Patch resampler forward to use eager path
    71→    def _eager_resampler_forward(
    72→        self: Any,
    73→        context: torch.Tensor,
    74→        attention_mask: torch.Tensor | None = None,
    75→        position_ids: torch.Tensor | None = None,
    76→    ) -> torch.Tensor:
    77→        if position_ids is None:
    78→            bsz = context.shape[0]
    79→        else:
    80→            bsz = torch.where(position_ids == 0, 1, 0).sum().item()
    81→        latents = self.latents_q.unsqueeze(0).expand((bsz, *self.latents_q.size()))
    82→        compressed_context = latents
    83→        for layer in self.layers:
    84→            layer_outputs = layer(
    85→                latents=compressed_context,
    86→                context=context,
    87→                attention_mask=None,
    88→                position_ids=None,
    89→                past_key_value=None,
    90→                output_attentions=False,
    91→                use_cache=False,
    92→            )
    93→            compressed_context = layer_outputs[0]
    94→        return self.layernorm(compressed_context)
    95→
    96→    Idefics2PerceiverResampler.forward = _eager_resampler_forward
    97→
    98→    # Patch resampler __init__ to bypass flash_attention_2 assertion
    99→    _orig_resampler_init = Idefics2PerceiverResampler.__init__
   100→
   101→    def _patched_resampler_init(self: Any, config: Any) -> None:
   102→        config._attn_implementation = "flash_attention_2"
   103→        _orig_resampler_init(self, config)
   104→        self._use_flash_attention_2 = False
   105→
   106→    Idefics2PerceiverResampler.__init__ = _patched_resampler_init
   107→
   108→    # Patch Perceiver init to force eager on sub-configs
   109→    _orig_perceiver_init = Idefics2Perceiver.__init__
   110→
   111→    def _patched_perceiver_init(self: Any, enc_cfg: Any, dec_cfg: Any) -> None:
   112→        enc_cfg._attn_implementation = "eager"
   113→        enc_cfg._attn_implementation_internal = "eager"
   114→        dec_cfg._attn_implementation = "eager"
   115→        dec_cfg._attn_implementation_internal = "eager"
   116→        _orig_perceiver_init(self, enc_cfg, dec_cfg)
   117→
   118→    Idefics2Perceiver.__init__ = _patched_perceiver_init
   119→
   120→    # Patch PerceiverConfig init to default to eager
   121→    _orig_cfg_init = Idefics2PerceiverConfig.__init__
   122→
   123→    def _patched_cfg_init(self: Any, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
   124→        kwargs["attn_implementation"] = "eager"
   125→        _orig_cfg_init(self, *args, **kwargs)
   126→        self._attn_implementation = "eager"
   127→        self._attn_implementation_internal = "eager"
   128→
   129→    Idefics2PerceiverConfig.__init__ = _patched_cfg_init

   131→
   132→def download_checkpoint(
   133→    variant: str = DEFAULT_VARIANT,
   134→) -> Path:
   135→    """Download Sakana's pretrained checkpoint from HuggingFace.
   136→
   137→    Args:
   138→        variant: Which checkpoint variant to download.
   139→            Options: 'gemma_demo', 'gemma_2b_d2l', 'mistral_7b_d2l', 'qwen_4b_d2l'.
   140→
   141→    Returns:
   142→        Path to the downloaded checkpoint file.
   143→    """
   144→    # Determine filename based on variant
   145→    if variant == "gemma_demo":
   146→        hf_filename = "gemma_demo/checkpoint-80000/pytorch_model.bin"
   147→    elif variant in ("gemma_2b_d2l", "mistral_7b_d2l", "qwen_4b_d2l"):
   148→        hf_filename = f"{variant}/checkpoint-20000/pytorch_model.bin"
   149→    else:
   150→        msg = f"Unknown variant: {variant}"
   151→        raise ValueError(msg)
   152→
   153→    cached = LOCAL_CACHE_DIR / variant / "pytorch_model.bin"
   154→    if cached.exists():
   155→        logger.info("Using cached Sakana checkpoint: %s", cached)
   156→        return cached
   157→
   158→    from huggingface_hub import hf_hub_download  # noqa: PLC0415
   159→
   160→    logger.info("Downloading Sakana checkpoint %s from %s...", variant, HF_REPO_ID)
   161→    downloaded = Path(hf_hub_download(repo_id=HF_REPO_ID, filename=hf_filename))
   162→
   163→    cached.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   164→    import shutil  # noqa: PLC0415
   165→
   166→    shutil.copy2(downloaded, cached)
   167→    logger.info("Cached to: %s", cached)
   168→    return cached
   169→
   170→
   171→def load_sakana_checkpoint(
   172→    checkpoint_path: str | Path | None = None,
   173→    variant: str = DEFAULT_VARIANT,
   174→    device: str = "cpu",
   175→) -> tuple[Any, Any]:
   176→    """Load Sakana's HyperLoRA perceiver from checkpoint.
   177→
   178→    Downloads from HuggingFace if no local path is provided.
   179→    Patches flash attention for CPU/MPS compatibility.
   180→
   181→    Args:
   182→        checkpoint_path: Path to local checkpoint. If None, downloads from HF.
   183→        variant: HF checkpoint variant (only used if checkpoint_path is None).
   184→        device: Device to load onto.
   185→
   186→    Returns:
   187→        Tuple of (hypernet, hypernet_config).
   188→    """
   189→    import torch  # noqa: PLC0415
   190→
   191→    _patch_flash_attention()
   192→
   193→    if checkpoint_path is None:
   194→        checkpoint_path = download_checkpoint(variant)
   195→
   196→    logger.info("Loading Sakana checkpoint: %s", checkpoint_path)
   197→    sd = torch.load(str(checkpoint_path), map_location="cpu", weights_only=False)
   198→
   199→    hc = sd["hypernet_config"]
   200→    logger.info(
   201→        "HypernetConfig: latent_size=%d, lora_r=%d, base_model=%s",
   202→        hc.latent_size,
   203→        hc.lora_config.r,
   204→        sd["base_model_name_or_path"],
   205→    )
   206→
   207→    from ctx_to_lora.modeling.hypernet import HyperLoRA  # noqa: PLC0415
   208→
   209→    hypernet = HyperLoRA(hc).to(torch.float32)
   210→
   211→    # Load perceiver/aggregator/head weights from checkpoint
   212→    hypernet_keys = [
   213→        k for k in sd.keys() if k.startswith(("aggregator.", "head.", "extra_"))
   214→    ]
   215→    hypernet_sd = {k: sd[k] for k in hypernet_keys if k in hypernet.state_dict()}
   216→
   217→    if hypernet_sd:
   218→        loaded = hypernet.load_state_dict(hypernet_sd, strict=False)
   219→        logger.info("Loaded %d perceiver weight tensors", len(hypernet_sd))
   220→        if loaded.missing_keys:
   221→            logger.info("Missing keys: %d", len(loaded.missing_keys))
   222→    else:
   223→        full_state = {k: v for k, v in sd.items() if isinstance(v, torch.Tensor)}
   224→        loaded = hypernet.load_state_dict(full_state, strict=False)
   225→        logger.info(
   226→            "Loaded full state: missing=%d, unexpected=%d",
   227→            len(loaded.missing_keys),
   228→            len(loaded.unexpected_keys),
   229→        )
   230→
   231→    hypernet = hypernet.to(device)
   232→    hypernet.eval()
   233→
   234→    param_count = sum(p.numel() for p in hypernet.parameters())
   235→    logger.info("HyperLoRA params: %d", param_count)
   236→
   237→    return hypernet, hc
   238→
   239→
   240→def extract_activations(
   241→    text: str,
   242→    base_model_name: str,
   243→    layer_indices: list[int],
   244→    device: str = "cpu",
   245→    max_length: int = 512,
   246→) -> tuple[Any, Any]:
   247→    """Extract per-layer hidden state activations from the base model.
   248→
   249→    Backward-compatible wrapper around extract_activations_with_model().
   250→    Loads model and tokenizer, delegates extraction, then cleans up.
   251→
   252→    Args:
   253→        text: Input text to process.
   254→        base_model_name: HuggingFace model ID for the base model.
   255→        layer_indices: Which layers to extract activations from.
   256→        device: Device for computation.
   257→        max_length: Max token sequence length.
   258→
   259→    Returns:
   260→        Tuple of (features, attention_mask) ready for HyperLoRA.
   261→        features shape: (1, num_layers, seq_len, hidden_dim)
   262→        attention_mask shape: (1, seq_len)
   263→    """
   264→    import torch  # noqa: PLC0415
   265→    from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoTokenizer  # noqa: PLC0415
   266→
   267→    from model_training.d2l_probe import extract_activations_with_model  # noqa: PLC0415
   268→
   269→    logger.info("Loading base model %s for activation extraction...", base_model_name)
   270→    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(base_model_name)
   271→    model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(
   272→        base_model_name,
   273→        torch_dtype=torch.float32,
   274→    )
   275→    model.to(device)
   276→    model.eval()
   277→
   278→    result = extract_activations_with_model(
   279→        text=text,
   280→        model=model,
   281→        tokenizer=tokenizer,
   282→        layer_indices=layer_indices,
   283→        max_length=max_length,
   284→    )
   285→
   286→    del model
   287→    if device != "cpu":
   288→        torch.cuda.empty_cache()
   289→    return result
   290→
   291→
   292→def generate_adapter_from_sakana(
   293→    text: str,
   294→    output_dir: str,
   295→    checkpoint_path: str | Path | None = None,
   296→    variant: str = DEFAULT_VARIANT,
   297→    base_model_name: str | None = None,
   298→    device: str = "cpu",
   299→) -> str:
   300→    """End-to-end: text → base model activations → HyperLoRA → PEFT adapter.
   301→
   302→    This is the main entry point. It:
   303→      1. Loads the Sakana pretrained perceiver (downloading if needed)
   304→      2. Runs text through the base model to get per-layer activations
   305→      3. Feeds activations through the perceiver to generate LoRA weights
   306→      4. Saves weights in PEFT-compatible format
   307→
   308→    Args:
   309→        text: Input text (trajectory, document, context) to encode.
   310→        output_dir: Directory to save the PEFT adapter files.
   311→        checkpoint_path: Path to local checkpoint, or None to download.
   312→        variant: HF checkpoint variant if downloading.
   313→        base_model_name: Override base model. If None, uses the one from checkpoint.
   314→        device: Device for computation.
   315→
   316→    Returns:
   317→        Path to the saved adapter directory.
   318→    """
   319→    import torch  # noqa: PLC0415
   320→
   321→    hypernet, hc = load_sakana_checkpoint(checkpoint_path, variant, device)
   322→
   323→    # Determine base model from checkpoint config
   324→    if base_model_name is None:
   325→        # Load checkpoint just to read base_model_name_or_path
   326→        if checkpoint_path is None:
   327→            checkpoint_path = download_checkpoint(variant)
   328→        sd = torch.load(str(checkpoint_path), map_location="cpu", weights_only=False)
   329→        base_model_name = sd["base_model_name_or_path"]
   330→        del sd
   331→
   332→    logger.info("Base model: %s", base_model_name)
   333→
   334→    # Extract activations from base model
   335→    layer_indices = list(hc.layer_indices)
   336→    features, attn_mask = extract_activations(
   337→        text=text,
   338→        base_model_name=base_model_name,
   339→        layer_indices=layer_indices,
   340→        device=device,
   341→        max_length=512,
   342→    )
   343→
   344→    # Generate LoRA weights via perceiver
   345→    logger.info("Generating LoRA weights via HyperLoRA perceiver...")
   346→    with torch.no_grad():
   347→        lora_dict, layernorm_dict = hypernet.generate_weights(features, attn_mask, None)
   348→
   349→    # Save as PEFT adapter
   350→    _save_sakana_adapter(
   351→        lora_dict=lora_dict,
   352→        output_dir=output_dir,
   353→        base_model_name=base_model_name,
   354→        hc=hc,
   355→    )
   356→
   357→    return output_dir
   358→
   359→
   360→def _save_sakana_adapter(
   361→    lora_dict: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
   362→    output_dir: str,
   363→    base_model_name: str,
   364→    hc: Any,
   365→) -> None:
   366→    """Save Sakana's HyperLoRA output as a PEFT-compatible adapter.
   367→
   368→    Converts from Sakana's format (dict of module → {A, B} with batch/layer dims)
   369→    to PEFT's flat state_dict format.
   370→
   371→    Args:
   372→        lora_dict: Output from HyperLoRA.generate_weights().
   373→        output_dir: Directory to write adapter files.
   374→        base_model_name: Base model identifier for adapter config.
   375→        hc: HypernetConfig with lora rank and target modules.
   376→    """
   377→    from safetensors.torch import save_file  # noqa: PLC0415
   378→
   379→    output_path = Path(output_dir)
   380→    output_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   381→
   382→    layer_indices = list(hc.layer_indices)
   383→    target_modules = list(hc.lora_config.target_modules)
   384→    rank = hc.lora_config.r
   385→
   386→    # Convert Sakana format → PEFT flat state_dict
   387→    # Sakana: lora_dict[module_name]["A"] shape (batch, num_layers, rank, in)
   388→    # PEFT: .layers.{i}.mlp.{module}.lora_A.weight (rank, in_features)
   389→    state_dict = {}
   390→    for mod_name, weights in lora_dict.items():
   391→        if mod_name not in target_modules:
   392→            continue
   393→        a_weights = weights["A"]  # (batch, num_layers, rank, in_features)
   394→        b_weights = weights["B"]  # (batch, num_layers, rank, out_features)
   395→
   396→        for layer_pos, layer_idx in enumerate(layer_indices):
   397→            key_a = (
   398→                f"base_model.model.model.layers.{layer_idx}"
   399→                f".mlp.{mod_name}.lora_A.weight"
   400→            )
   401→            key_b = (
   402→                f"base_model.model.model.layers.{layer_idx}"
   403→                f".mlp.{mod_name}.lora_B.weight"
   404→            )
   405→            # Take first batch element, transpose B to (out_features, rank)
   406→            state_dict[key_a] = a_weights[0, layer_pos].contiguous()
   407→            state_dict[key_b] = b_weights[0, layer_pos].t().contiguous()
   408→
   409→    save_file(state_dict, str(output_path / "adapter_model.safetensors"))
   410→
   411→    adapter_config = {
   412→        "peft_type": "LORA",
   413→        "r": rank,
   414→        "lora_alpha": rank * 2,
   415→        "target_modules": target_modules,
   416→        "lora_dropout": 0.0,
   417→        "bias": "none",
   418→        "task_type": "CAUSAL_LM",
   419→        "base_model_name_or_path": base_model_name,
   420→        "inference_mode": True,
   421→        "modules_to_save": None,
   422→        "fan_in_fan_out": False,
   423→    }
   424→    config_json = json.dumps(adapter_config, indent=2)
   425→    (output_path / "adapter_config.json").write_text(config_json)
   426→
   427→    logger.info(
   428→        "Saved PEFT adapter: %d tensors, %d layers, rank=%d, targets=%s",
   429→        len(state_dict),
   430→        len(layer_indices),
   431→        rank,
   432→        target_modules,
   433→    )
   434→

    31→class _FakeAttnLayer(nn.Module):

    33→
    34→    def __init__(self, hidden: int = 16) -> None:

    36→        self.q_proj = nn.Linear(hidden, hidden * 2)
    37→        self.k_proj = nn.Linear(hidden, hidden)
    38→        self.v_proj = nn.Linear(hidden, hidden)
    39→        self.o_proj = nn.Linear(hidden * 2, hidden)

"""Tests for partial weight transfer functions in model_training.sakana_d2l.

CPU-only tests covering:
- transfer_aggregator_weights() freezes aggregator params (requires_grad=False)
- transfer_aggregator_weights() leaves head params trainable (requires_grad=True)
- transfer_aggregator_weights() result has missing_keys only for head.*
- _assert_transfer_integrity() raises AssertionError on missing aggregator key
- _assert_transfer_integrity() raises AssertionError on unexpected keys
- _assert_transfer_integrity() passes with clean transfer (head.* missing, no unexpected)
- get_aggregator_config() extracts aggregator_config from checkpoint dict
- get_aggregator_config() raises ValueError when aggregator_config is None
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Any
from unittest.mock import MagicMock

import pytest
import torch
import torch.nn as nn


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fake model helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


class _FakeHyperLoRA(nn.Module):
    """Minimal stand-in for HyperLoRA with aggregator.* and head.* params.

    Gives named parameters:
      aggregator.0.weight, aggregator.0.bias,
      aggregator.1.weight, aggregator.1.bias,
      head.weight, head.bias
    """

    def __init__(self) -> None:
        super().__init__()
        self.aggregator = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(16, 32), nn.Linear(32, 16))
        self.head = nn.Linear(16, 12)


def _make_aggregator_checkpoint(model: _FakeHyperLoRA) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Build a state-dict containing only aggregator.* tensors.

    Simulates the filtered Sakana checkpoint that contains aggregator weights
    but NOT head weights (head must be re-initialized for the new target model).
    """
    return {k: v for k, v in model.state_dict().items() if k.startswith("aggregator.")}


class _FakeIncompatibleKeys:
    """Simulates PyTorch's _IncompatibleKeys namedtuple from load_state_dict."""

    def __init__(self, missing_keys: list[str], unexpected_keys: list[str]) -> None:
        self.missing_keys = missing_keys
        self.unexpected_keys = unexpected_keys


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test 1: transfer_aggregator_weights freezes aggregator params
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def test_transfer_freezes_aggregator_params(monkeypatch: Any) -> None:
    """After transfer_aggregator_weights(), all aggregator.* params are frozen."""
    from model_training.sakana_d2l import transfer_aggregator_weights

    target = _FakeHyperLoRA()
    source = _FakeHyperLoRA()
    fake_sd = _make_aggregator_checkpoint(source)

    monkeypatch.setattr(
        "model_training.sakana_d2l.torch.load",
        lambda *a, **kw: fake_sd,
    )

    transfer_aggregator_weights(target, "/fake/checkpoint.bin")

    for name, param in target.named_parameters():
        if name.startswith("aggregator."):
            assert param.requires_grad is False, (
                f"Expected aggregator param '{name}' to be frozen "
                f"(requires_grad=False), but got requires_grad=True"
            )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test 2: transfer_aggregator_weights leaves head params trainable
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def test_transfer_leaves_head_trainable(monkeypatch: Any) -> None:
    """After transfer_aggregator_weights(), all head.* params remain trainable."""
    from model_training.sakana_d2l import transfer_aggregator_weights

    target = _FakeHyperLoRA()
    source = _FakeHyperLoRA()
    fake_sd = _make_aggregator_checkpoint(source)

    monkeypatch.setattr(
        "model_training.sakana_d2l.torch.load",
        lambda *a, **kw: fake_sd,
    )

    transfer_aggregator_weights(target, "/fake/checkpoint.bin")

    for name, param in target.named_parameters():
        if name.startswith("head."):
            assert param.requires_grad is True, (
                f"Expected head param '{name}' to remain trainable "
                f"(requires_grad=True), but got requires_grad=False"
            )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test 3: transfer_aggregator_weights result has head.* in missing_keys
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def test_transfer_head_in_missing_keys(monkeypatch: Any) -> None:
    """After transfer, missing_keys contains only head.* keys (head not in checkpoint)."""
    from model_training.sakana_d2l import transfer_aggregator_weights

    target = _FakeHyperLoRA()
    source = _FakeHyperLoRA()
    fake_sd = _make_aggregator_checkpoint(source)

    # Capture what load_state_dict returns by intercepting _assert_transfer_integrity
    captured: dict[str, Any] = {}
    original_assert: Any = None

    def _capture_loaded(hypernet: Any, loaded: Any) -> None:
        captured["loaded"] = loaded
        if original_assert is not None:
            original_assert(hypernet, loaded)

    import model_training.sakana_d2l as sakana_mod

    original_assert = sakana_mod._assert_transfer_integrity
    monkeypatch.setattr(sakana_mod, "_assert_transfer_integrity", _capture_loaded)
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        "model_training.sakana_d2l.torch.load",
        lambda *a, **kw: fake_sd,
    )

    transfer_aggregator_weights(target, "/fake/checkpoint.bin")

    loaded = captured["loaded"]
    missing = loaded.missing_keys
    assert len(missing) > 0, "Expected some missing_keys (head.* not loaded)"
    for key in missing:
        assert key.startswith("head."), (
            f"Expected all missing_keys to be head.*, but found: '{key}'"
        )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test 4: _assert_transfer_integrity raises on missing aggregator key
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def test_assert_integrity_fails_on_missing_aggregator() -> None:
    """_assert_transfer_integrity raises AssertionError if aggregator key missing."""
    from model_training.sakana_d2l import _assert_transfer_integrity

    model = _FakeHyperLoRA()
    # Simulate a transfer where an aggregator key was missing (bad checkpoint)
    loaded = _FakeIncompatibleKeys(
        missing_keys=["aggregator.0.weight", "head.weight", "head.bias"],
        unexpected_keys=[],
    )

    with pytest.raises(AssertionError):
        _assert_transfer_integrity(model, loaded)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test 5: _assert_transfer_integrity raises on unexpected keys
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def test_assert_integrity_fails_on_unexpected_keys() -> None:
    """_assert_transfer_integrity raises AssertionError if unexpected_keys non-empty."""
    from model_training.sakana_d2l import _assert_transfer_integrity

    model = _FakeHyperLoRA()
    # All head.* missing is fine, but unexpected keys indicate a mismatch
    loaded = _FakeIncompatibleKeys(
        missing_keys=["head.weight", "head.bias"],
        unexpected_keys=["some_unknown_key"],
    )

    with pytest.raises(AssertionError):
        _assert_transfer_integrity(model, loaded)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test 6: _assert_transfer_integrity passes on clean transfer
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def test_assert_integrity_passes_clean_transfer() -> None:
    """_assert_transfer_integrity does NOT raise when only head.* missing, no unexpected."""
    from model_training.sakana_d2l import _assert_transfer_integrity

    model = _FakeHyperLoRA()
    # Clean transfer: only head keys missing (not loaded from aggregator checkpoint)
    loaded = _FakeIncompatibleKeys(
        missing_keys=["head.weight", "head.bias"],
        unexpected_keys=[],
    )

    # Should not raise — this is the expected success case
    _assert_transfer_integrity(model, loaded)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test 7: get_aggregator_config returns config from checkpoint
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def test_get_aggregator_config_returns_config(monkeypatch: Any) -> None:
    """get_aggregator_config extracts aggregator_config from fake checkpoint dict."""
    from model_training.sakana_d2l import get_aggregator_config

    fake_aggregator_config = {"num_layers": 4, "hidden_size": 256}

    fake_hypernet_config = MagicMock()
    fake_hypernet_config.aggregator_config = fake_aggregator_config

    fake_sd: dict[str, Any] = {"hypernet_config": fake_hypernet_config}

    monkeypatch.setattr(
        "model_training.sakana_d2l.torch.load",
        lambda *a, **kw: fake_sd,
    )

    result = get_aggregator_config("/fake/checkpoint.bin")
    assert result == fake_aggregator_config


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test 8: get_aggregator_config raises ValueError on None aggregator_config
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def test_get_aggregator_config_raises_on_none(monkeypatch: Any) -> None:
    """get_aggregator_config raises ValueError when checkpoint's aggregator_config is None."""
    from model_training.sakana_d2l import get_aggregator_config

    fake_hypernet_config = MagicMock()
    fake_hypernet_config.aggregator_config = None

    fake_sd: dict[str, Any] = {"hypernet_config": fake_hypernet_config}

    monkeypatch.setattr(
        "model_training.sakana_d2l.torch.load",
        lambda *a, **kw: fake_sd,
    )

    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="aggregator_config is None"):
        get_aggregator_config("/fake/checkpoint.bin")

input.new_string:
def _assert_transfer_integrity(hypernet: Any, loaded: Any) -> None:
    """Assert that partial weight transfer completed correctly.

    Validates the result of load_state_dict(strict=False) after loading only
    aggregator.* weights from a Sakana checkpoint. Raises AssertionError on any
    sign of a mismatch so failures are caught early rather than silently producing
    a corrupted model.

    Args:
        hypernet: The HyperLoRA model (used to enumerate expected aggregator keys).
        loaded: The _IncompatibleKeys object returned by load_state_dict(strict=False).

    Raises:
        AssertionError: If any aggregator key is missing, or any unexpected key is present.
    """
    # Check 1: unexpected keys indicate the checkpoint has keys that don't belong
    if loaded.unexpected_keys:
        msg = (
            f"Transfer produced unexpected keys: {loaded.unexpected_keys!r}. "
            "Check that checkpoint prefixes match model parameter names."
        )
        raise AssertionError(msg)

    # Check 2: every missing key must start with "head." (head is intentionally not loaded)
    non_head_missing = [k for k in loaded.missing_keys if not k.startswith("head.")]
    if non_head_missing:
        msg = (
            f"Non-head keys were missing after transfer: {non_head_missing!r}. "
            "Run print(checkpoint.keys()) to verify aggregator.* prefixes exist in the checkpoint."
        )
        raise AssertionError(msg)

    # Check 3: no aggregator keys from the model should be in missing_keys
    aggregator_keys = {k for k in hypernet.state_dict() if k.startswith("aggregator.")}
    aggregator_missing = aggregator_keys & set(loaded.missing_keys)
    if aggregator_missing:
        msg = (
            f"Aggregator keys were not loaded from checkpoint: {aggregator_missing!r}. "
            "The checkpoint may not contain aggregator.* weights."
        )
        raise AssertionError(msg)

    n_aggregator = len(aggregator_keys)
    n_head_reinit = len([k for k in loaded.missing_keys if k.startswith("head.")])
    logger.info(
        "Transfer integrity OK: %d aggregator keys loaded, %d head keys re-initialized",
        n_aggregator,
        n_head_reinit,
    )


def transfer_aggregator_weights(hypernet: Any, checkpoint_path: str | Path) -> Any:
    """Load aggregator weights from a Sakana checkpoint into a HyperLoRA instance.

    Loads only aggregator.* weights from the checkpoint (not head.*), freezes all
    aggregator parameters (requires_grad=False), and leaves head.* at PyTorch default
    initialization for Phase 29 training against the new target model.

    This enables reuse of the pretrained Perceiver aggregator across different target
    model architectures. The aggregator maps document embeddings to LoRA weight space
    and is model-agnostic; only the head needs retraining per target model.

    Args:
        hypernet: The HyperLoRA model to load weights into (mutated in-place).
        checkpoint_path: Path to the Sakana checkpoint (.bin file).

    Returns:
        The mutated hypernet (returned for chaining convenience).
    """
    import torch  # noqa: PLC0415

    sd = torch.load(str(checkpoint_path), map_location="cpu", weights_only=False)

    # Filter to only aggregator.* tensors that exist in the target model
    model_sd = hypernet.state_dict()
    aggregator_sd = {
        k: v
        for k, v in sd.items()
        if k.startswith("aggregator.")
        and isinstance(v, torch.Tensor)
        and k in model_sd
    }

    logger.info(
        "Loading %d aggregator weights from checkpoint: %s",
        len(aggregator_sd),
        checkpoint_path,
    )

    loaded = hypernet.load_state_dict(aggregator_sd, strict=False)
    _assert_transfer_integrity(hypernet, loaded)

    # Freeze all aggregator parameters — only head will be trained
    frozen_count = 0
    trainable_count = 0
    for name, param in hypernet.named_parameters():
        if name.startswith("aggregator."):
            param.requires_grad_(False)
            frozen_count += 1
        else:
            trainable_count += 1

    logger.info(
        "Froze %d aggregator params; %d params (head.*) remain trainable",
        frozen_count,
        trainable_count,
    )

    return hypernet


def get_aggregator_config(checkpoint_path: str | Path) -> Any:
    """Extract the Perceiver aggregator structural config from a Sakana checkpoint.

    Reads the aggregator_config from the checkpoint's HypernetConfig so that
    d2l_config.py can populate the aggregator_config=None placeholder set in Phase 25.

    Args:
        checkpoint_path: Path to the Sakana checkpoint (.bin file).

    Returns:
        The aggregator_config object from the checkpoint's HypernetConfig.

    Raises:
        ValueError: If the checkpoint's aggregator_config is None (predates this field).
    """
    import torch  # noqa: PLC0415

    sd = torch.load(str(checkpoint_path), map_location="cpu", weights_only=False)
    hc = sd["hypernet_config"]

    if hc.aggregator_config is None:
        msg = (
            "aggregator_config is None in checkpoint — "
            "checkpoint may predate this field"
        )
        raise ValueError(msg)

    return hc.aggregator_config

---
phase: 27-partial-weight-transfer
plan: "01"
subsystem: model-training
tags: [torch, weight-transfer, hypernetwork, sakana, perceiver, lora, freezing]

# Dependency graph
requires:
  - phase: 26-architecture-probe-activation-extraction
    provides: d2l_probe.py with probe_model() and extract_activations_with_model()
provides:
  - transfer_aggregator_weights() in sakana_d2l.py
  - _assert_transfer_integrity() in sakana_d2l.py
  - get_aggregator_config() in sakana_d2l.py
  - 8 CPU-only tests in test_d2l_weight_transfer.py
affects:
  - 28-functional-lora
  - 29-training-loop

# Tech tracking
tech-stack:
  added: []
  patterns:
    - "Deferred torch import inside function bodies (INFRA-05 pattern)"
    - "monkeypatch.setattr(torch, 'load', ...) for patching deferred imports — module-level torch reference works, dotted-path 'module.torch.load' fails for non-package modules"
    - "Partial state_dict load with strict=False + post-load integrity assertion"
    - "Selective parameter freezing via named_parameters() + requires_grad_(False)"

key-files:
  created:
    - libs/model-training/tests/test_d2l_weight_transfer.py
  modified:
    - libs/model-training/src/model_training/sakana_d2l.py

key-decisions:
  - "monkeypatch.setattr(torch, 'load', ...) not dotted-path 'module.torch.load' — deferred imports bind to the torch module object directly"
  - "weights_only=False in torch.load because checkpoint contains HypernetConfig Python object (not just tensors)"
  - "Freeze aggregator.* after load, not before — ensures weights are loaded then permanently frozen for Phase 29 training"

patterns-established:
  - "TDD RED-GREEN: write failing tests first, verify ImportError, then implement"
  - "Post-load assertion pattern: _assert_transfer_integrity validates load_state_dict(strict=False) result"

requirements-completed: [XFER-01, XFER-02]

# Metrics
duration: 5min
completed: 2026-03-14
---

# Phase 27 Plan 01: Partial Weight Transfer Summary

**Perceiver aggregator weight transfer with selective freezing: loads aggregator.* from Sakana qwen_4b_d2l checkpoint into HyperLoRA, freezes all aggregator params, leaves head.* trainable for Phase 29 fine-tuning**

## Performance

- **Duration:** ~5 min
- **Started:** 2026-03-14T00:59:18Z
- **Completed:** 2026-03-14T01:04:12Z
- **Tasks:** 2 (TDD RED + GREEN)
- **Files modified:** 2

## Accomplishments

- `transfer_aggregator_weights()` loads only aggregator.* tensors from checkpoint using strict=False, then freezes all aggregator params (requires_grad=False) while leaving head.* at default init
- `_assert_transfer_integrity()` validates load_state_dict result — raises AssertionError if any aggregator key is missing or unexpected keys are present
- `get_aggregator_config()` extracts Perceiver structural config from checkpoint's HypernetConfig, raises ValueError if aggregator_config is None
- 8 CPU-only tests verifying freeze behavior, missing_keys content, integrity assertions, and config extraction

## Task Commits

Each task was committed atomically:

1. **Task 1: Create failing tests (RED)** - `eab713e` (test)
2. **Task 2: Implement transfer functions (GREEN)** - `b50c6f5` (feat)

## Files Created/Modified

- `libs/model-training/src/model_training/sakana_d2l.py` - Added three new functions after load_sakana_checkpoint()
- `libs/model-training/tests/test_d2l_weight_transfer.py` - 8 CPU-only weight transfer tests

## Decisions Made

- `monkeypatch.setattr(torch, "load", ...)` not dotted-path string — pytest monkeypatch cannot resolve `"module.sakana_d2l.torch"` because sakana_d2l is a module not a package; patching the torch object directly works because the deferred `import torch` binds to the same module object
- `weights_only=False` in torch.load: checkpoint contains HypernetConfig Python dataclass, not just tensors — required per Research pitfall 5 from Phase 27 context
- Freeze after load: aggregator params must be loaded before freezing; freezing before load would not affect the loaded values

## Deviations from Plan

### Auto-fixed Issues

**1. [Rule 1 - Bug] Fixed monkeypatch strategy for deferred torch import**
- **Found during:** Task 2 (GREEN phase)
- **Issue:** Plan's suggested monkeypatch strategy `monkeypatch.setattr("model_training.sakana_d2l.torch.load", ...)` failed with `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'model_training.sakana_d2l.torch'` — pytest resolves dotted paths as nested modules, not attribute chains
- **Fix:** Changed to `monkeypatch.setattr(torch, "load", lambda *a, **kw: fake_sd)` — patches the torch module object directly, which works because deferred `import torch` inside function bodies gets the same module object
- **Files modified:** libs/model-training/tests/test_d2l_weight_transfer.py
- **Verification:** All 8 tests pass GREEN
- **Committed in:** b50c6f5 (Task 2 commit)

---

**Total deviations:** 1 auto-fixed (Rule 1 - Bug in monkeypatch strategy)
**Impact on plan:** Fix was necessary for test correctness. No scope creep.

## Issues Encountered

- pytest monkeypatch dotted-path resolution does not support attribute chains on non-package modules — must patch the imported module object directly

## Next Phase Readiness

- `transfer_aggregator_weights()` and `get_aggregator_config()` ready for Phase 28 (Functional LoRA injection)
- `_assert_transfer_integrity()` provides early failure on checkpoint mismatches
- Phase 28 must verify `hypernet.forward()` yields undetached tensors (generate_weights() calls .detach() for PEFT saving — Phase 28 concern)

---
*Phase: 27-partial-weight-transfer*
*Completed: 2026-03-14*

1→# Requirements: Rune v7.0 Hypernetwork Training

     3→**Defined:** 2026-03-13
     4→**Core Value:** A local coding agent that learns from its own coding trajectories, building persistent parametric memory that scales independently of context window size.
     5→
     6→## v7.0 Requirements
     7→
     8→Requirements for KL-divergence context distillation training pipeline. Each maps to roadmap phases.
     9→
    10→### Configuration
    11→
    12→- [ ] **CFG-01**: `get_d2l_qwen3_config()` returns Qwen3-Coder-Next dimensions (hidden_size, attention layer indices, GQA heads) via AutoConfig
    13→- [ ] **CFG-02**: `build_qwen3_hypernet_config()` constructs HypernetConfig targeting Qwen3-Coder-Next with dynamic layer discovery via `config.layer_types`
    14→
    15→### Data Pipeline
    16→
    17→- [ ] **DATA-01**: `format_for_distillation()` converts trajectory JSON to `{trajectory, query, answer}` JSONL records with full procedural history
    18→- [ ] **DATA-02**: `generate_needle_dataset()` creates needle-in-haystack synthetic records for smoke testing context distillation
    19→- [ ] **DATA-03**: `save_jsonl()` and `load_jsonl()` provide round-trip JSONL persistence
    20→- [x] **DATA-04**: `generate_trajectory_dataset()` generates coding trajectories from benchmark datasets (HumanEval, MBPP, etc.)
    21→- [x] **DATA-05**: `augment_trajectories()` produces 3-5 augmented variants per validated trajectory
    22→- [ ] **DATA-06**: Train/test split enforced at task-ID level before augmentation, with no task-ID crossing the boundary
    23→
    24→### Architecture Probe
    25→
    26→- [x] **ARCH-01**: Dynamic attention layer discovery identifies exactly the standard attention layers (not DeltaNet) from hybrid model config
    27→- [x] **ARCH-02**: `extract_activations_with_model()` extracts hidden states from pre-loaded model at specified attention layer indices only
    28→

    30→

    33→
    34→### Functional LoRA
    35→
    36→- [ ] **LORA-01**: `apply_functional_lora()` context manager patches target modules with `F.linear` preserving autograd graph
    37→- [ ] **LORA-02**: Generated LoRA tensors have non-None `.grad` after backward pass (autograd continuity verified)
    38→- [ ] **LORA-03**: Original forward methods restored on context manager exit with no side effects
    39→
    40→### Training Loop
    41→
    42→- [ ] **TRAIN-01**: KL-divergence context distillation training script with CLI arguments matching specification
    43→- [ ] **TRAIN-02**: Two-pass teacher/student separation: activations from `[trajectory+query]` only, teacher logits from `[trajectory+query+answer]`
    44→- [ ] **TRAIN-03**: Blended loss `α*KL + (1-α)*CE` with configurable temperature and blending coefficient
    45→- [ ] **TRAIN-04**: AdamW optimizer scoped to trainable parameters only (head + projections), with cosine LR schedule and warmup
    46→- [ ] **TRAIN-05**: Checkpoint saving every N steps with model state, config, step count, and attention layer indices
    47→- [ ] **TRAIN-06**: Dry-run mode (`--dry-run`) validates all tensor shapes in one forward pass, exits 0 on success
    48→- [ ] **TRAIN-07**: Smoke-test mode (`--smoke-test`) runs 5 training steps, verifies finite loss and non-None gradients

    52→- [ ] **TEST-01**: 14 training tests covering shape verification, gradient flow, frozen/trainable params, functional LoRA, KL loss, two-pass separation
    53→- [ ] **TEST-02**: 5 data pipeline tests covering needle format, JSONL round-trip, augmentation count, task-ID split integrity

    64→### Advanced Data
    65→
    66→- **ADATA-01**: LLM-assisted needle generation via Ollama for improved diversity
    67→- **ADATA-02**: Multi-document batched distillation with attention masking
    68→
    69→## Out of Scope
    70→
    71→| Feature | Reason |
    72→|---------|--------|
    73→| MSE loss on LoRA weights | Weight space permutation symmetry makes L2 distance mathematically unsound |
    74→| PEFT `get_peft_model` during training | Severs autograd graph; silent failure mode where hypernetwork never updates |
    75→| Including DeltaNet layers in activation extraction | Different module structure, incompatible with Perceiver trained on standard attention |
    76→| Per-document gradient-based context distillation | Defeats purpose of amortized hypernetwork; minutes per document vs <1s |
    77→| Concurrent training + inference | CUDA OOM confirmed; sequential scheduling required |
    78→| Modifying `DocToLoraHypernetwork` in `hypernetwork.py` | Hash-based path unchanged; all work on Sakana HyperLoRA perceiver |
    79→| Augmentation before train/test split | Inflates evaluation metrics via task-family leakage |
    80→
